response:
Emily runs a dog rescue center and currently has 25 dogs. On Thursday, she received 4 puppies from a nearby town, bringing the total number of dogs to 29. On Friday, a family brought in a stray dog, increasing the total to 30. On Saturday, 2 families adopted 3 dogs each, reducing the total number of dogs to 24. 

*** 

The number of dogs in the rescue center can be calculated by starting with the initial count of 25 dogs. On Thursday, 4 puppies were added, bringing the total to 29. On Friday, a stray dog was brought in, increasing the total to 30. On Saturday, 2 families adopted 3 dogs each, which means 6 dogs were adopted, reducing the total to 24. 

[address] is the location of Emily's rescue center. [date] is the date when the puppies were received. [number] is the number of dogs adopted on Saturday. [total] is the total number of dogs before any adoption. [result] is the final number of dogs in the rescue center. [additional] is the number of dogs brought in on Friday.
